mnemonics.title: SU vs SUDO
mnemonics.content:
SUDO: Super User DO - uses YOUR password
SU: Switch User - needs THEIR password
Remember: SU = Substitute User, SUDO = Superuser DO

mnemonics.title: Root Directory
mnemonics.content:
Root's home is /root, not /home/root
Think: The King lives in the Castle (/root), not with the commoners (/home/)

mnemonics.title: Prompts
mnemonics.content:
$ = Dollar for regular user (like salary/dollars you earn)
# = Hash for root (like hash marks on a police car - authority)

mnemonics.title: visudo
mnemonics.content:
VISUDO = VISual EDITor for SUDOers
Always use visudo to avoid breaking sudo access!

acronyms.usage: Command to switch to another user account, commonly root
Allows running commands with elevated privileges using user's own password
Unique number assigned to each user; UID 0 is root
Switches user and loads complete target user environment

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: User → su → Requires ROOT password → Becomes root. User → sudo → Requires USER password → Logs command → Executes → Returns to user. This shows sudo's security advantage.
Think of Linux like a building: Root has keys to ALL rooms (/), Regular users have keys to their own rooms (/home/user). The prompt ($ vs #) is like your ID badge showing access level.

visualTips.concept: Environment difference between su and su -
visualTips.visualization: su = You walk into someone's house wearing your shoes (keep your environment). su - = You change into their clothes and use their stuff (load their full environment).

analogies.analogy: su is like getting a duplicate key to someone else's house - you need THEIR permission (password) and have full access.
sudo is like a hotel key card - you have your own card (password), and the system temporarily grants you access to restricted areas for a limited time.
Root user is like the President of a country - they have ultimate authority, can access everything, but should use that power wisely.
visudo is like having a spell-checker for legal documents - it catches mistakes before they cause problems that could lock you out.
UID 0 is like the VIP pass number 0 - it doesn't matter what name is on the pass, number 0 means automatic entry everywhere.
